scenario_title: Duplicate add of the same RR in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified case where a single UPDATE carries two identical add_rr records for the same TXT RR. The zone is preloaded so the RRset already exists with exactly the same RR, making the duplicate insertion semantically redundant. A set-normalizing implementation can accept the message and leave the RRset unchanged, while another implementation may treat the repeated add as a conflict and return YXRRSET. Querying the name after the UPDATE provides a stable observable for whether the zone state remains the expected single RRset entry.

group_1.behavior: Accepts the UPDATE as a harmless duplicate insertion and normalizes to a single TXT RR in the rrset.
group_1.post_state: dup.campus.edu. TXT present as one logical RRset containing "v".

group_2.behavior: Rejects the redundant second add as a conflicting or superfluous operation on an existing RRset.
group_2.post_state: dup.campus.edu. TXT remains as initially loaded, with no net change from the UPDATE.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines how RRs are added in UPDATE but does not specify duplicate normalization or whether repeated identical add_rr operations inside one message must be merged or rejected. RFC 2181 §5.1 treats an RRset as a set, but it does not define UPDATE algorithm behavior for redundant insertions. As a result, servers may differ on whether the second identical add is a no-op or an error, producing different replies and potentially different observable post-state handling.
